channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Broken shelves dumped outside gates on Merrow street, 7th May
item.description:
Broken shelving dumped by the gates to flats on Merrow street. Looks like exposed screws, nails are poking out and into the pavement area

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Merrow Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of leaves, flowers dropping, greenfly affecting garden, 7th May
item.description:
I am reporting a tree behind 77 Forburg Road, N16 6HR.The tree is causing ongoing nuisance due to excessive leaf and flower fall into my garden. There is also a persistent issue with greenfly/insects affecting the area.please urgently inspect the tree.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Osbaldeston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On the green in Orient street SE11 4SR, 7th May
item.description:
Dumped old large sofa

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Orient Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Root problems previously raised has not been resolved, 7th May
item.description:
The cherry tree roots are now severely damaging my front wall. I'm concerned about the costs to repair my wall. Until a root barrier is installed (as previously requested) or the tree cut back/removed, then I will loose the wall completley.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Hurstwood Avenue, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Green bin in way, 7th May
item.description:
Moved in the pavement again

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mina Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Daily tipping still here and added to, 7th May
item.description:
3 days and this keeps getting bigger..its the same people.it need investigating not clearing up every time as this is just assisting them and not us dutiful residents

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton High Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside salvation army, 7th May
item.description:
2 suitcases left

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chichester Road, London

item.title: Dumped matteess, 7th May
item.description:
Dumped mattress since last week at the end of Morston Gardens. People leaving dog poo bags on top now too.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mells Crescent, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parking over drop down and blocking the road, 7th May
item.description:
People constantly block the drop down when picking people up making it impossible for the elderly and disabled. A further issue is blocking half of the road when parking making it dangerous to exit and enter both Iceland and TFLs carpark. Request a yellow box and double yellows with cameras
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Station Square, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped chair, 7th May
item.description:
Someone has dumped a red chair on the corner of Norway Gate and Redriff Road. Please could this be collected as soon as possible. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Norway Gate, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Opposite Kilburn Station, entering pavement on Maygrove Road, 7th May
item.description:
Tree not listed on the map. Opposite Kilburn Station at the entrance of Maygrove Road the trees are obstructing the pavement. The trees that overhang are covered in sap, so are really sticky. It's a nightmare when I have to pass through with my pram. Please can Camden cut this back?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2-118, Maygrove Road, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned van or someone living in it, 7th May
item.description:
For some time, this van had been left here, with a visor completely covering (every inch) of the windscreen. Given its May, and parked under trees, no reason except something dodgy for this to be here. Quite possibly someone is living in this van. A year or so ago in same place another van was parked similarly (six months+). Needs to be investigated as looks like a dodgy activity is taking place here as well as being an eyesore

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lytchet Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattresses dumped, 7th May
item.description:
End of Windus Walk

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Oldhill Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped mattress, 7th May
item.description:
Please collect this dumped mattress

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 51, Linnell Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Tree, 7th May
item.description:
As reported before this tree is clearly dieing/dead as per photos. It lost all its leaves last year months and months before any other trees lost theirs. It has ivy growing up it and has failed to grow any leaves this Spring at all and is barren. Im not sure why nothing was done last year when this was reported. It is now worse. As per photos, It is out of place with no leaves and has much ivy growth up the trunk and has some black marks on it. I have had a tree surgeon and gardeners stop me when I am outside my property and ask if I want it felling as it is clearly dead, but it isn’t owned by me, it is owned by the council. I believe this dead tree should be felled, it is very large and I have concerns about my property if it becomes unstable. Its branches overhang my property. My neighbours have expressed to me that the tree is dead and also asked about why it isn’t being dealt with by the council.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Magpie Hall Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly posting with coded anti semitic undertones, 7th May
item.description:
Despite the horror that was Epstein this is a thinly veiled racist poster which may also be inciting violence - ‘wipe out’ - against Jews at a time of rising anti semitism.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kennington Park Road, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
